element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn to build facilities on planets to mine minerals, organics, and radioactive materials

element_introduction_order.element: Simple ship construction and fleet assembly
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Building basic ships for defense and exploration before advanced combat

element_introduction_order.element: Warp point navigation and system exploration
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Understanding star system layout and connected warp routes to expand territory
Encountering different alien races with varying aggression profiles (Xi-Chung aggressive early, Praetorian peaceful but expansionist)
Turn-by-turn research point allocation to unlock better facilities and ship components
Optional but inevitable as rivals expand; direct planetary destruction and fleet battles
Alternative win condition through peaceful diplomatic allying with all races
Managing fleets through unpredictable star system dangers that destroy ships and systems
Access to exclusive tech lines (Organic, Crystalline, Psychic, Temporal, Deeply Religious) providing asymmetric power
Technologies enabling planet destruction, star manipulation into black holes or storms

typical_tension_triggers: Encountering aggressive races early (Xi-Chung hive threat)
Rival technological breakthroughs advancing their military capability
Resource competition on newly discovered planets
Fleet encounters with rival forces at warp points
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based strategy with layered tension: early-game expansion pressure from naturally aggressive races (Xi-Chung) contrasts with mid-game technological arms race where peaceful races (Praetorian, Eee) gradually become formidable. Late-game features catastrophic planetary manipulation technologies that raise existential stakes. Tension escalates with game progression as rivals consolidate power and scientific advancement accelerates.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Star system composition varies (ice/rock/gas planets, asteroids, storms, multiple stars); higher condition quality planets and richer mineral deposits signal valuable targets. Supernova-prone systems discourage settlement.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Systems connected by warp points form hidden expansion paths; player exploration of warp network reveals strategic chokepoints and resource-rich isolated regions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Manageable resource management and colony building, but Xi-Chung aggressive races create immediate military pressure. Players learn peaceful expansion vs. early military engagement tradeoff.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Technological arms race intensifies. Peaceful races like Praetorian and Eee gain sophistication despite slower start. Multiple simultaneous threats: rival expansion, stellar hazards, research competition.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Catastrophic scale warfare becomes possible with planet-destroying and star-manipulating technologies. Survivor count stabilizes as rival empires are eliminated. Win conditions require either military dominance or unprecedented diplomatic coordination.
notable_spikes: First contact with naturally aggressive alien race (Xi-Chung) in early game
Mid-game transition when peaceful races unlock their advanced technologies
Late-game emergence of planetary/stellar manipulation weapons
End-game technological singularity if one player achieves research supremacy

notable_level_patterns.description: Game generates discrete star systems with variable composition (planet types, atmospheres, mineral content, hazards, warp connectivity). Each scenario presents unique strategic geography without traditional level design.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Standard multiplayer game with random galaxy seed

notable_level_patterns.description: Different alien races have inherent tendencies (Xi-Chung = early aggression; Praetorian = peaceful expansion; Eee = intelligence/late-game tech advantage) creating emergent difficulty curves within same map.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any multi-race game where early Xi-Chung threat differs from late-game Eee threat

notable_level_patterns.description: Large technology tree with multiple advancement paths and racial tech specializations creates exponential strategic variation. Early research choices constrain late-game options, encouraging varied playstyles.
